🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a pre-revenue European regulated-intermediary SaaS startup building DORA or IDD compliance tooling for insurance distribution at pre-seed stage.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European fintech startup integrating with Open Banking or PSD2 APIs to automate payment reconciliation or AML screening for regulated payment providers.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Luminance (anchor)
2. Lexi (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 4 companies → evaluated: 1 for Profund VC

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

in 5d — Sat May 23Saturday:insurance distribution digital platform SaaS Europe founding engineer @ Europe
in 6d — Sun May 24Sunday:regtech MiFID DORA compliance SaaS Europe B2B seed founding team @ Europe
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 14 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads
Apify raw: 18 → no URL: -2 → passed to LLM: 14

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

Quantinuum10NO MATCHQuantinuum operates in quantum computing, which is not aligned with either the AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech angles. It also appears more focused on enterprise rather than specific SME/mid-market European teams and lacks a clear French market focus.https://www.quantinuum.com/startup-partner-programsifted.eu/feed
Aqxolt0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1.https://www.zaubacorp.com/AQXOLT-INDIA-PRIVATE-LIMsifted.eu/feed
Healx10NO MATCHHealx is a biotech company focused on drug discovery for rare diseases, which does not align with either the AI workflow automation for business operations or the regulated intermediary fintech criteria. It fails STEP 1 as it is not a B2B software product primarily sold to professional or institutional buyers within the specified verticals.https://healx.ai/about/sifted.eu/feed
PolyAI20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company provides an omnichannel platform for customer interactions using advanced AI, which is a general-purpose chatbot/conversational AI solution. This does not align with the specific workflow automation needs for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor does it target regulated intermediaries as described in the thesis angles. It is closer to a general-purpose chatbot without a vertical workflow focus, which is a reject criterion for ai_workflow_automation.https://poly.ai/aboutsifted.eu/feed
N8n30NO MATCHThe company provides a general workflow automation tool and while it could be used for various B2B functions, there is no evidence it is specifically AI-native (LLM-powered, agent-based, or generative AI co-pilot) or that it targets French or European SMEs/mid-market companies. The description also lacks mention of specific European or French market traction.https://n8n.io/pricing/startup-plan/sifted.eu/feed

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 197 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
Lightfield30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. Lightfield builds software to help founders understand their customers and market, which is a general business intelligence or knowledge management tool, not specifically an AI workflow automation for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries.https://lightfield.app/company
Seapoint30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/market) fails. While Seapoint offers business accounts with automation for payroll, expenses, and reporting, which could align with the 'finance teams' aspect of the ai_workflow_automation angle, there is no mention of AI-native features (LLM-powered, agent-based, generative AI co-pilot), nor is there any indication of a focus on France or the French-speaking European market, which is a core requirement for both angles. The company is based in Dublin.https://f4.fund/startups/seapoint
Wise10NO MATCHThe company, Startup Wise Guys, is an accelerator program, not a B2B software product company as required by GATE QUESTION A. It supports startups across various verticals, including SaaS and Fintech, but does not itself provide software.https://startupwiseguys.com/
9fin30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed because 9fin is an AI-native information platform for global debt markets, which is not an AI-native workflow automation product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams at European SMEs/mid-market companies, nor is it B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries in France or the EU automating regulatory reporting/KYC/client onboarding.https://www.9fin.com/resources
Allica Bank0NO MATCHSTEP A fails. Allica Bank is a business bank offering current accounts, savings, and lending services, not a B2B software product (SaaS, AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology).https://www.allica.bank/
Cleo30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (ai_workflow_automation) requires a focus on France/French-speaking European market, which is not supported by the company description. The company does not align with the other active angle (regulated_intermediary_fintech) either.https://www.cleo.com/home
Cusp AI10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. CuspAI designs breakthrough materials using generative AI, which is a deep tech/materials science application, not an AI-native workflow automation product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams in the specified verticals, nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries.https://startupintros.com/orgs/cuspai
Cal.com Platform20NO MATCHThe company provides a platform for building scheduling businesses, which is not an AI-native workflow automation product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams (ai_workflow_automation) nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries (regulated_intermediary_fintech). Fails STEP B.https://cal.com/docs/platform/introductionv7_3
Product Landscapes0NO MATCHThe company provides a checklist and tips for starting a landscaping business, which does not align with B2B software, AI workflow automation, or regulated intermediary fintech. It appears to be a content/resource site rather than a SaaS product.https://www.landscapersapp.com/blog/landscaping-buv7_3
Findr: remember everything10NO MATCHThe company, Personal.ai (formerly Human AI), is developing a personal AI designed to remember all information fed to it, acting as a 'digital clone' to supplement the user's memory. This is a B2C productivity tool and consumer AI product, which is explicitly in the REJECT CRITERIA for STEP 1, and does not align with the B2B focus of the thesis.https://dot.la/personal-ai-2659213500.htmlv7_3

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

🟪 90/100 Morclear Europe

sforbt
1 MATCH · 65 NO MATCH · 2% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
2. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 2d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 3d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 4d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 5d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 6d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 7d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 8d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 9d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 10d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 11d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 12d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 13d — Sun May 31“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 14d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 15d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 16d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 17d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 18d — Fri Jun 5“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 19d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 20d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
in 21d — Mon Jun 8“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
in 22d — Tue Jun 9“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 23d — Wed Jun 10“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 24d — Thu Jun 11“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 25d — Fri Jun 12“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 26d — Sat Jun 13“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
in 27d — Sun Jun 14“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”

ZTL30NO MATCHThe company provides B2B API-based payment solutions but does not specifically focus on corporate travel payments, hotel invoicing, or VAT recovery for corporate travel programs, as required by the 'corporate_travel_payments' angle. YowPay15NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. YowPay provides a turnkey payments solution for general merchants across e-commerce and physical storefronts, focused on SEPA Instant transfers. It is not specifically within the European corporate travel technology domain, nor does it specialize in hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://yowpay.com
Payaut20NO MATCHNuvei provides payment processing infrastructure that is general-purpose, not specifically tailored for corporate travel payment processing or VAT recovery for European corporate travel programs as required by the 'corporate_travel_payments' angle. Its description does not indicate travel-specific reconciliation or features, thus failing element 'A' of the gate question which requires a CORE business within European corporate travel tech and alignment with at least one active angle.https://nuvei.com
Afterbanks Arcopay10NO MATCHAfterbanks Arcopay appears to be a financial technology company specializing in open banking payments, facilitating direct bank transfers and payment processing. This does not align with any of the specified angles (hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments specifically for travel, or travel ERP integrations) but rather seems to be a generic payment solution. Therefore, it fails STEP 1, as its core business is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://afterbanks.com
WithLess90MATCHThe company's core business is a fintech-ops platform that automates finance processes, including expense management, reconciliation, and ERP integration, specifically mentioning corporate cards and policy enforcement. This aligns with the 'corporate_travel_payments' and 'travel_erp_integrations' angles as it provides B2B SaaS or fintech infrastructure for automated invoice capture and ERP integration for expenses. The description explicitly mentions multi-country operations and examples within Europe, satisfying the European focus.https://withless.com
Alpa10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Alpa provides financial management software specifically for the hospitality industry (hotels, restaurants), focusing on profit & loss statements and financial insights. It is neither a corporate travel technology company nor does it align with any of the specified angles (hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or travel ERP integrations). Its financial tools are for hospitality operations, not corporate travel management.https://getalpa.com
Lilo20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Lilo is a B2B procurement platform for hotels and hospitality businesses, focusing on managing hotel supplies, vendors, invoices, and spend for the hotels themselves, not specifically for corporate travel booking, management, or payment.https://lilohq.com
Veetal30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. While Veetal offers B2B solutions for the hotel industry, its focus on white-label RMS and data APIs for hotels and travel agencies (OTAs, consultancies, etc.) does not align with the European corporate travel technology domain as defined by the thesis. It lacks clear evidence of automating or digitizing hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration specifically for business travel.https://veetal.app
Su API25NO MATCHThe company provides a white-label API-driven channel management and GDS platform for property management systems and vacation rental/hotel operators, focusing on distribution to OTAs and agents. This is a B2B SaaS for property owners/PMS, not a corporate travel tech solution for booking, payments, or TMC workflows, failing to meet the core business domain of corporate travel technology (STEP A).https://su-api.com
reconcileOTA30NO MATCHStep A fails because reconcileOTA's core business is reconciling discrepancies between hotels and OTAs to recover funds, which is a niche accounting/revenue recovery service for hotels, not directly related to European corporate travel technology infrastructure (booking, payments, workflow, or ERP integration for corporate clients/TMCs). It is not focused on the defined angles within the corporate travel domain.https://reconcileota.com
StructurrAI30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. StructurrAI's core business is 'AI-native infrastructure for hotel and room mapping and website-grade content enrichment' for OTAs, bedbanks, and DMCs, not explicitly for corporate travel and policy enforcement, payments, or TMC workflows. While it serves travel platforms, its focus on content enrichment and mapping is a horizontal travel tech infrastructure play, not specifically corporate travel, and does not align with core business travel functions required by the thesis.https://structurrai.com
XQuic10NO MATCHThe company's core business is hotel back-office automation and revenue recovery, which is not within the specified European corporate travel technology domains of hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel defined in STEP A. It is a hotel-specific operational tool, not a corporate travel tool.https://xquic.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Expensya (anchor)
2. Jenji (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

This does not align with any of the specified European corporate travel technology angles such as hotel booking, TMC workflow, or travel payments. It fails STEP A of the gate question.https://lexi.hr
Parahelp0DATA_GAPLLM error — defaulting to manual reviewhttps://parahelp.com
Solvea0NO MATCHSolvea provides an AI-powered resolution engine for general customer support, clearly not operating within the European corporate travel technology domain (STEP A), nor aligning with any specific angle such as hotel booking, TMC workflow, payments, or ERP integrations for business travel.https://solvea.cx
Tymely0NO MATCHThe company provides an AI-powered customer service agent for retailers, which is unrelated to European corporate travel technology. It fails STEP A of the GATE QUESTION.https://tymely.ai
Lastbot20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because LastBot provides an AI-powered customer communication platform for SMEs, focusing on general customer service, marketing, and sales automation using AI agents. This is a generic business automation tool and does not align with the European corporate travel technology domain.https://lastbot.com
Abby10NO MATCHThe company's core business is a micro-business management platform for invoicing, financial management, automated declarations to state agencies, and project organization. This is a generic financial and business management tool for micro-entrepreneurs, not specifically within the European corporate travel technology domain. Therefore, it fails STEP A. It is not possible for it to align with any of the specific angles.https://abby.fr
Easy Expense10NO MATCHThe company provides a small business expense tracking app, which is a generic finance tool, not specifically focused on European corporate travel technology as required by STEP A. It does not align with any of the specific angles.https://easy-expense.com
Juggl10NO MATCHThe company provides payroll and workforce management software, which is a generic HR and finance tool, not specifically focused on corporate travel technology as required by STEP A. While it handles payroll, this does not equate to corporate travel payments or ERP integrations for travel.https://juggl.co
Tomhrm10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Tomhrm is an HR platform with modules for recruiting, onboarding, time tracking, and performance management. This is a generic HR tool, not specifically focused on European corporate travel technology.https://tomhrm.com
Hibob0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. Hibob is an HR software company, not a corporate travel technology company. It focuses on HR, payroll, and finance processes, which is a generic HR/finance tool, not specific to business travel.https://hibob.com
Payfit10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) failed. The company's core business is cloud-based payroll and HR management, not European corporate travel technology. It does not automate or digitize hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration specifically for business travel.https://payfit.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 4 companies → evaluated: 1 for sforbt
ALL 32 QUERIES — sforbt (all active every day):
QUERIES BY ANGLE (32 total · 0/day prod · 0/day test per thesis):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 8 queries
1. “"corporate hotel booking" platform startup Europe Series A”
2. “"hotel sourcing" SaaS "travel manager" Europe funding”
3. “"online booking tool" hotel corporate Europe acquisition”
4. “"preferred hotel program" software startup B2B”
5. “"hotel aggregator" corporate travel Europe SaaS”
6. “"réservation hôtelière" entreprise logiciel startup France”
7. “"business hotel" booking platform TMC integration Europe”
8. “"hotel RFP" software procurement travel managers startup”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 8 queries
1. “"TMC software" OR "travel management company platform" startup Europe funding”
2. “"mid-office travel" automation SaaS Europe startup Series A”
3. “"travel agency" workflow automation B2B software Europe startup”
4. “"TMC" "back-office" SaaS startup Europe acquisition”
5. “"logiciel TMC" OR "agence voyages d'affaires" startup France SaaS”
6. “"travel servicing" automation platform Europe seed raise”
7. “TMC workflow automation Europe startup investment 2024 2025”
8. “"booking fulfillment" travel automation software B2B Europe”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 8 queries
1. “"virtual card" hotel corporate travel Europe startup funding 2024 2025”
2. “"VAT recovery" travel "business travel" Europe SaaS startup”
3. “"hotel invoice" automation "AI" corporate travel Europe startup”
4. “"lodging payment" reconciliation B2B fintech Europe raise”
5. “"récupération TVA" voyage affaires startup France SaaS”
6. “corporate travel payments fintech Europe Series A acquisition”
7. “"travel expense" reconciliation hotel invoice automation Europe startup”
8. “"PCI" hotel payment processing corporate travel SaaS Europe”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 8 queries
1. “"travel ERP" integration SaaS Europe startup funding 2024”
2. “"SAP" OR "Oracle" travel booking integration connector Europe startup”
3. “"back-office" travel integration middleware European startup Series A”
4. “"TMC" data integration ERP connector B2B software Europe”
5. “"intégration ERP" voyages affaires startup France SaaS”
6. “corporate travel data middleware Europe seed funding startup”
7. “"travel booking" SAP integration automation SaaS Europe acquisition”
8. “hotel spend ERP connector European startup raise investment”

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.
LinkedIn Posts — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 16 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 14 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 197 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
Hacker News — Show HN (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches Hacker News (Algolia index) for Show HN and Ask HN posts matching your sector keywords. Finds technical founders launching products in your niche. Free, runs daily.

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.
